title: ensure_account(0) SQL write on every PaymentAction::Request
description: Every incoming PaymentAction::Request triggers ensure_account(0) which executes INSERT OR IGNORE INTO accounts — a SQL write even when the row already exists. SQLite's INSERT OR IGNORE acquires a write lock regardless of whether the insert is a no-op. At low payment volume this is negligible. The fix is to call ensure_account(0) once after try_load_wallet_fvks succeeds, not on every payment request. The account row is guaranteed to exist for the lifetime of the session after that single call.
